title: Fix and complete Deeds platform features

requirements.summary: Fix Memory Jars feature: enable saving deeds from others into a personal scrapbook with public/private toggle and notifications
acceptanceCriteria: Memory Jar entries appear in the MemoryJarView component with saved posts displayed as polaroid-style cards
Users can save a post to their Memory Jar from the feed via the PostCard action button
Memory Jar has a public/private toggle accessible from the profile page
A notification is sent to the original poster when their deed is saved to someone's Memory Jar
MemoryJarPostCard renders correctly with photo/video/caption and author info
Remove button on MemoryJarPostCard deletes the entry from the jar

file_operations.description: Add a 'Save to Memory Jar' action button to the PostCard component. Wire the button to call a mutation that saves the post to the current user's memory jar. Display appropriate feedback toast on success or error. Ensure the button is visible and styled consistently with other action buttons (like, comment, share).

Update MemoryJarView to fetch and display all saved memory jar entries using the useQuery hook. Ensure entries are rendered in a responsive grid of MemoryJarPostCards. Add loading skeleton states while data is fetching. Implement an empty state when no entries exist. Add a public/private visibility toggle at the top of the view that calls a backend mutation to update the jar's visibility setting.

Update MemoryJarPostCard to properly render the saved post with polaroid-style layout: display photo/video at the top, caption below, and author info at the bottom. Add a remove button (X or trash icon) in the top-right corner that calls a mutation to delete the entry from the memory jar. Fetch and display the original author's profile data. Ensure responsive design and hover effects.

Add a Memory Jar section/tab to the Profile component. Include a link or button that navigates to or toggles the MemoryJarView display. Ensure the Memory Jar section is visible and accessible alongside other profile sections like posts, albums, and notifications.

Add query hooks for fetching memory jar entries: useGetMemoryJar() to fetch all entries for the current user, and useGetMemoryJarVisibility() to get the public/private setting. Add mutation hooks: useSaveToMemoryJar(postId) to save a post, useRemoveFromMemoryJar(entryId) to delete an entry, and useUpdateMemoryJarVisibility(isPublic) to toggle visibility. Implement proper error handling and cache invalidation on mutations.

requirements.summary: Fix Profile page: ensure it loads and displays avatar, posts, albums, followers/following, notifications, memory jar, and profile/status options
acceptanceCriteria: Profile page renders without a blank screen for authenticated users
Avatar, display name, bio, follower count, and following count are visible
User's posts are listed on the profile page
Profile picture upload button is present and functional
Status update button opens the UpdateStatusModal
Memory Jar section is accessible from the profile page

file_operations.description: Debug and fix the Profile component to ensure it renders correctly for authenticated users. Verify that useGetCallerUserProfile() is called and data is properly awaited before rendering. Add comprehensive error boundary handling for profile data loading failures. Ensure all sections (avatar, bio, posts, albums, followers/following counts, notifications, memory jar) are wired and displayed. Add proper loading states using skeleton placeholders. Verify that the profile picture upload button and status update button are present and trigger their respective modals (UpdateStatusModal for status, file input dialog for avatar upload). Test with both empty and populated user profiles.

Review and ensure useGetCallerUserProfile() query hook properly handles all loading, error, and success states. Add retry logic for failed profile fetches. Ensure the query returns user profile data including avatar, displayName, bio, follower/following counts, and posts. Verify that dependent queries (posts, albums, notifications) are enabled only after the profile is successfully loaded. Add query key dependencies to prevent stale data issues.

requirements.summary: Fix deed categories: implement dynamic category assignment with randomized options from a curated pool
acceptanceCriteria: When the CreatePostModal opens, a set of category options is displayed for the user to choose from
Category options are randomized/rotated from a curated pool of deed-related categories (e.g., Kindness, Community, Family, Health, Environment, Creativity, Education, Support)
Selected category is saved with the post and displayed as a badge on PostCard
Categories do not fail to load or show empty states

file_operations.description: Define a curated pool of deed categories as a constant array: ['Kindness', 'Community', 'Family', 'Health', 'Environment', 'Creativity', 'Education', 'Support', 'Compassion', 'Service', 'Growth', 'Connection']. When the modal opens, randomly select 4-6 categories from the pool using array shuffle logic. Display the randomized categories as selectable buttons or a dropdown in the form. Ensure the selected category is included in the post data when submitted. Add validation to require category selection before submission. Store the selected category in local component state.

Update PostCard to display the post's category as a badge near the top of the card. Style the badge with a distinct background color (e.g., light blue or gradient) and rounded corners. Ensure the badge is visible for all posts that have a category. Handle posts without a category gracefully by not rendering the badge.

Update the useCreatePost mutation hook to accept a category field as part of the post creation payload. Ensure the category is passed to the backend when creating a post. No backend-specific logic should be mentioned here; focus on frontend data handling and API call structure.

requirements.summary: Implement Profile Report Card: weekly activity summary with user-selectable grading scale and public/private toggle
acceptanceCriteria: A Report Card section appears on the profile page
The card displays weekly stats: number of posts, deeds completed, likes received, and kindness matches
User can select their preferred grading scale (letter, star, or percentage) from a settings toggle
The computed grade/score is displayed prominently on the card based on the selected scale
A public/private toggle controls whether the report card is visible to other users
Report card data refreshes weekly (based on the current week's activity)

file_operations.description: Create a new ReportCard component that displays the user's weekly activity summary. Fetch weekly stats (posts count, deeds completed, likes received, kindness matches count) using a custom query hook. Display the stats in a card layout with clear labels. Implement a grading scale selector (dropdown or button group) with options: Letter (A-F), Star (1-5 stars), Percentage (0-100%). Compute the grade based on total weekly activity points using a scoring algorithm (e.g., A = 90-100 points, B = 80-89, etc.). Display the computed grade prominently at the top of the card. Add a public/private toggle switch that calls a mutation to update visibility. Style the card with a report-card-inspired design (e.g., bordered sections, academic theme). Include a week indicator showing the current week's date range.

Add the ReportCard component to the Profile page layout. Position it below the user info section and above the posts feed. Ensure it is only visible for the current user's own profile. Pass necessary user data as props to the ReportCard component.

Add a query hook useGetWeeklyReportCard() that fetches the current user's weekly activity stats from the backend. Add mutation hooks: useUpdateReportCardGradingScale(scale) to save the user's preferred grading scale (letter/star/percentage), and useUpdateReportCardVisibility(isPublic) to toggle public/private visibility. Implement cache invalidation on mutations to refresh the report card display.

requirements.summary: Implement Deeds Bar on profile page: 19-hour daily activity tracker with animated icons on a lightning background
acceptanceCriteria: Deeds Bar component is rendered exclusively on the Profile page
The bar displays a light baby blue lightning background
Floating bear, pineapple, and monkey head emoji/icon elements animate within the bar
The bar visually represents progress through a 19-hour daily usage window using the useDeedsUsage hook data
Progress indicator updates as the user's session time accumulates throughout the day

file_operations.description: Implement the DeedsBar component to display a horizontal progress bar representing the user's daily app usage against a 19-hour limit. Set the background to the generated light baby blue lightning pattern using the workspace path frontend/public/assets/generated/lightning-bg.dim_800x400.png. Overlay floating animated icons: bear (frontend/public/assets/generated/bear-icon.dim_128x128.png), pineapple (frontend/public/assets/generated/pineapple-icon.dim_128x128.png), and monkey (frontend/public/assets/generated/monkey-icon.dim_128x128.png). Use CSS animations or keyframes to make the icons float/bounce within the bar boundaries. Use the useDeedsUsage hook to get current session time and daily total. Calculate progress percentage as (dailyTotal / (19 * 60 * 60 * 1000)) * 100. Render a progress fill element that grows from left to right based on the percentage. Display time remaining or elapsed in a small label. Ensure the bar is responsive and fits within the profile page layout without overflow.

Add the DeedsBar component to the Profile page layout. Position it prominently near the top, below the header/avatar section but above the posts feed. Ensure it only renders on the Profile route and is visible to the current user viewing their own profile.

requirements.summary: Implement lock screen overlay when daily 19-hour usage limit is reached, with interactive dismissal
acceptanceCriteria: A full-screen overlay appears automatically when daily usage reaches 19 hours (tracked by useDeedsUsage hook)
The overlay is shown on every page/route when the limit is reached
The overlay contains a message about the daily limit with key terms displayed in bold
Clicking any bold letter word in the message dismisses the overlay and allows the user to continue
Dismissal state is stored in sessionStorage so the overlay does not re-appear until the next day
The overlay has a visually distinct design consistent with the Deeds app aesthetic

file_operations.description: Create a new LockScreenOverlay component that renders a fixed full-screen overlay (z-index: 9999, backdrop blur). Use the useDeedsUsage hook to check if daily usage >= 19 hours. Display a centered card with a message explaining the daily limit (e.g., 'You've reached your 19-hour daily usage limit. To continue, please accept our terms.'). Style key words in the message (e.g., 'accept', 'terms', 'continue') in bold. Make each bold word clickable by wrapping in a <span> or <button> element. On click of any bold word, store a dismissal flag in sessionStorage (key: 'lockScreenDismissed', value: current date string). Hide the overlay when dismissed. Check on component mount if dismissal flag exists and matches today's date; if so, do not show the overlay. Use a visually appealing design with gradient background and shadow effects consistent with the Deeds app theme.

Import and render the LockScreenOverlay component at the root level of the App component, outside the router outlet, so it appears on every route. Ensure it is rendered after authentication is established. The overlay component will manage its own visibility based on usage data and dismissal state.
